parentQuestion: A 55-year-old woman falls and fractures her hip. Six months later, the fracture ...

scaffolds.explain: Osteoblasts build bone by secreting osteoid (organic matrix) which then mineralizes. The suffix '-blast' means 'builder' or 'forming cell.' Osteoclasts destroy bone, osteocytes maintain it. Know your workers, darling.

scaffolds.q: What mineral is the main component of bone hardness?
scaffolds.explain: Bone is mineralized with hydroxyapatite crystals — calcium phosphate. This mineral gives bone its hardness and rigidity. The organic matrix (collagen) provides tensile strength, the mineral provides compressive strength. Beautiful engineering, darling.
